name: Vegetables Lo Mein
recipeInstructions:
1. Add soft noodles to boiling salted water; stir with chopsticks and cook until noodles lose their floury taste but are still firm. Immediately drain in a colander and rinse in cold water to arrest the cooking process. If you hold noodles for more than 10 minutes, toss them with a little oil to prevent sticking.
2. Soak Jyo mushrooms for 2 hours in warm water (1 hour for Nami mushrooms); slice into thin strips. Slice celery with the grain into thin strips about 3 inches long. Cut bamboo shoots into strips matching the celery.
3. Heat wok over medium-high heat. When hot, dribble oil around the side of the pan. Add salt; stir briefly.
4. Add mushrooms and bamboo shoots; stir for about 30 seconds. Add celery, stirring vigorously for 30 seconds.
5. Push vegetables to the side of the wok. Add broth and sugar, bringing to a boil.
6. Add noodles, a handful at a time, stirring them into the broth for about 20 seconds. Combine vegetables and noodles, cover the wok, and steam briefly (about 30 seconds).
7. Push all ingredients to one side. If necessary, thicken the juices slightly with cornstarch paste by adding it a little at a time while constantly stirring.
8. Serve immediately.
